name: Peanut Butter Banana Overnight Oats
why: Oats carry beta-glucan, a soluble fiber shown to lower LDL; peanut butter swaps in unsaturated fat.
ingredients: ½ cup soy or low-fat milk
steps: Stir oats, milk, peanut butter and chia together in a jar.
Top with banana and cinnamon.
Refrigerate overnight. Eat cold, or warm 60 seconds.

name: Berry Walnut Oatmeal
why: Soluble fiber from oats plus walnuts (omega-3 ALA) — both gently push LDL down.
ingredients: 1 cup soy or low-fat milk (or water)
steps: Simmer oats with milk 4–5 min, stirring.
Stir in berries for the last minute.
Top with walnuts and cinnamon.

name: Turmeric Tofu Scramble
why: Tofu is soy protein with zero cholesterol — a heart-smart stand-in for scrambled eggs.
steps: Sauté onion and pepper in olive oil until soft.
Add crumbled tofu, turmeric, cumin, salt and pepper; cook 5 min.
Fold in spinach until wilted. Serve on toast.

name: Red Lentil Dal with Rice
why: Lentils are packed with soluble fiber and plant protein, with essentially no saturated fat.
steps: Sauté onion, garlic and ginger in olive oil.
Add curry powder and cumin; stir 30 sec.
Add lentils, tomatoes and water; simmer 20 min until soft.
Stir in spinach if using. Serve over rice.

name: Black Bean Tacos
why: Beans plus avocado deliver soluble fiber and unsaturated fat; corn tortillas keep it light.
steps: Sauté onion and pepper until soft.
Add beans, cumin and chili powder; mash slightly and warm through.
Warm tortillas. Fill with beans, salsa, avocado and a squeeze of lime.

name: Chickpea & Spinach Curry
why: Tomato-based instead of coconut keeps saturated fat low while chickpeas pile on fiber.
steps: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil.
Add spices, then tomatoes and chickpeas; simmer 15 min.
Stir in spinach until wilted. Serve over rice.

name: Hearty Lentil & Vegetable Soup
why: A soluble-fiber powerhouse that makes a big batch and freezes beautifully.
steps: Sauté onion, carrot and celery in olive oil 5 min.
Add garlic and spices, then lentils, tomatoes and broth.
Simmer 30 min until lentils are tender. Remove bay leaf.

name: Minestrone
why: Beans, vegetables and whole-grain pasta; going light on cheese keeps saturated fat down.
steps: Sauté onion, carrot, celery and garlic in olive oil.
Add tomatoes, broth, beans and oregano; simmer 15 min.
Add pasta; cook until al dente.
Stir in greens until wilted.

name: Three-Bean Veggie Chili
why: Three kinds of beans stack soluble fiber; no meat means no saturated fat.
steps: Sauté onion, pepper and garlic in olive oil.
Stir in spices and tomato paste for 1 min.
Add tomatoes and all beans; simmer 25 min, stirring now and then.

name: Tofu & Broccoli Stir-Fry
why: Soy protein plus a pile of vegetables, with just a touch of oil.
steps: Brown tofu cubes in oil; set aside.
Stir-fry broccoli, pepper and carrot 4–5 min.
Add garlic, ginger, soy sauce and tofu; toss 2 min. Serve over rice.

name: White Beans & Greens Pasta
why: Olive oil and beans stand in for cream and cheese — fiber up, saturated fat down.
steps: Boil pasta; reserve ½ cup water before draining.
Gently sizzle garlic and red pepper in olive oil.
Add beans and greens until wilted, splash in pasta water.
Toss with pasta and lemon; finish with black pepper.

name: Stuffed Sweet Potato with Black Beans
why: Sweet potato and beans bring fiber and potassium — skip the sour cream and cheese.
steps: Bake sweet potatoes at 400°F / 200°C for 40 min until soft.
Warm beans and corn with cumin.
Split potatoes; pile on beans, corn, salsa, scallions and lime.

name: Lemon-Tahini Chickpea Quinoa Bowl
why: Quinoa and chickpeas for protein; tahini and olive oil are unsaturated fats.
steps: Whisk tahini, lemon, garlic, olive oil and a little water into a dressing.
Combine quinoa, chickpeas, cucumber, tomato and onion.
Toss with dressing.

name: Big Mediterranean Chickpea Salad
why: Olive oil and chickpeas make a fast, no-cook, heart-healthy lunch.
steps: Whisk olive oil, vinegar and oregano.
Toss everything together.
Season and serve.

name: Veggie Fried Brown Rice with Edamame
why: Edamame adds soy protein and fiber; one egg white keeps cholesterol negligible.
steps: Stir-fry carrot and pepper in oil 3 min.
Add garlic, edamame and rice; toss until hot.
Push aside; scramble egg white if using, then mix in.
Finish with soy sauce and scallions.

name: Hummus & Veg Plate or Wrap
why: A chickpea-and-tahini base with soluble fiber and unsaturated fat — snack or light lunch.
steps: Blend chickpeas, tahini, lemon, garlic, olive oil and a splash of water.
Serve with raw veg and pita — or roll into a wrap.

name: Light Lemon & Herb Chickpea Salad
why: Almost all fiber and protein with barely any fat — chickpeas and crisp veg make it filling for very few calories.
steps: Toss chickpeas, cucumber, tomato, onion and parsley together.
Dress with lemon, the teaspoon of olive oil, salt and pepper.
Chill 10 minutes if you have time — it's even better cold.
